initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of right ventricular hypertrophy?

criterion_selection.question: To determine which set of diagnostic criteria for right ventricular hypertrophy should be applied, which underlying conduction abnormality must be evaluated first?
options: Presence of left anterior fascicular block
Presence of right bundle branch block
Presence of left posterior fascicular block
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of right bundle branch block

finding_identification.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is right bundle branch block present on this ECG, including incomplete and complete RBBB?

diagnostic_decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this ECG suggest the presence of right ventricular hypertrophy?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
diagnostic_decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: Given that right bundle branch block is present, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated to diagnose right ventricular hypertrophy?
options: Presence of a normal QRS axis between -30 and 0 degrees
Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads

finding_identification.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the QRS axis greater than +60 degrees on this ECG?

ecg_grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you locate a QRS wave that would be used to calculate the QRS axis? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured QRS axis fall into?

diagnostic_decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this ECG suggest the presence of right ventricular hypertrophy?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
